metadata.summary: Map user stories 2D: backbone left-to-right (journey), tasks vertically by priority. Walking skeleton = MVP end-to-end.

content.title: User Story Mapping
content.summary: Arrange stories 2D: backbone (journey) left-to-right, tasks vertically by priority. Walking skeleton = one task per activity, end-to-end MVP. Use verb phrases; 5-10 activities.

why.p: Flat backlogs lose journey context—stories prioritized without full picture, critical steps disappear, scope becomes guesswork. Story map forces "How does backlog connect to user journey?" Walking skeleton defines earliest defensible MVP boundary, foundation for all slicing.

list.item: Decomposing product/feature with 3+ journey steps, shared mental model needed
Slicing releases from large backlog: walking skeleton then incremental slices
Discovery-delivery handoff: interview transcripts to structured backbone+tasks
Onboarding contributor who needs end-to-end understanding
Single isolated feature no journey (e.g. add endpoint)
Maintenance/bug-fix where journey already mapped and stable
Linear data-pipeline products with no user-facing journey

rule.statement: Use verb phrases for activities; left-to-right journey order; limit 5-10 items.
rule.rationale: Noun-only labels lose action context. 10+ collapses to Gantt, loses clarity.

example.title: E-commerce journey
example.note: Browse → View → Add Cart → Checkout → Receive Order

example.title: Nouns only
example.note: Products, Cart, Payment, Order — no journey verb

rule.statement: One task per backbone activity. Span every column—gaps mean journey cannot complete end-to-end.
rule.rationale: Missing column = MVP fails there. Completeness non-negotiable.

antipattern.description: Skeleton dropped because "nothing felt minimal" for one activity.
antipattern.reason: Activity exists because user must complete it. If no task minimal there, activity itself wrong.

rule.statement: Limit 3-5 release slices. Each delivers standalone user value.
rule.rationale: Dependent slice = phase, not release. 5+ slices destroys release thinking.

list.item: Backbone first in workshop with user voice (interviews count)
Always identify walking skeleton—no skeleton = no MVP
Limit slices to 3-5; more destroys thinking
Pair with mvp-scoping: skeleton ≈ Must, slice 1 ≈ MVP
Re-run after release using actual data—priorities change
Store as JSON/YAML queryable, not whiteboard image

template.purpose: Story map template: context, backbone, skeleton, slices
Story card with placement, AC, dependencies

script.purpose: Validate story-map JSON coverage and skeleton completeness
